title: Full Stack Developer
description: Step by step guide to becoming a full stack developer in 2025

topics.name: Ansible
topics.description: Ansible is an open-source configuration management, application deployment and provisioning tool that uses its own declarative language in YAML. Ansible is agentless, meaning you only need remote connections via SSH or Windows Remote Management via P

topics.name: Basic Aws Services
topics.description: AWS has several services but you don't need to know all of them. Some common ones that you can start with are EC2, VPC, S3, Route 53, and SES.

links.title: Up and Running with AWS VPC
Up and Running with AWS EC2

topics.name: Checkpoint / Collaborative Work
topics.description: Now that you have learnt git and GitHub you should be ready to work with others. You should now setup your GitHub profile and push all the projects that you have built so far to your GitHub profile. Here are some of my recommendations for your GitHub

topics.name: Checkpoint / External Packages
topics.description: At this point, you should be able to install and use external packages using `npm`. You probably know about [npmjs.com](https://npmjs.com/) where you can search for packages and read their documentation. You should also be familiar with the `package.

topics.name: Checkpoint / Frontend Apps
topics.description: At this point you should be able to build a complete frontend application including:

topics.name: Checkpoint / Interactivity
topics.description: At this point you should be able to add interactivity to your web pages using JavaScript. You should make sure that you have learnt the following:

topics.name: Checkpoint / Static Webpages
topics.description: Now that you have learnt HTML and CSS, you should be able to build static webpages. I recommend you to build as many test projects at each yellow step of the roadmap as possible to solidify what you learn.

topics.name: Checkpoint / Automation
topics.description: Now that you have learnt ansible, you can use it to automate the deployment of your application.

topics.name: Checkpoint / Ci / Cd
topics.description: Now that you have the infrastructure setup, it's time to automate the deployment process. This is where CI / CD comes in. If you don't know what CI/CD are, you should watch [DevOps CI/CD Explained in 100 Seconds](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=scEDH

topics.name: Checkpoint / Cli Apps
topics.description: At this point you should be able to build CLI applications using Node.js or whatever backend programming language you picked.

topics.name: Checkpoint / Complete App
topics.description: At this point, you should have everything that you need to build a complete application that:

topics.name: Checkpoint / Deployment
topics.description: Now that you know the basics of AWS, you should be able to deploy your application to AWS. You don't need to use all the AWS services, here is what you can probably get started with:

links.title: Deploy Node App on AWS EC2

topics.name: Checkpoint / Infrastructure
topics.description: If you remember, earlier in the roadmap, you manually logged into the AWS console and had to setup the services. Now that you know terraform, go ahead and automate the process of creating the infrastructure for your application using terraform and wi

topics.name: Checkpoint / Monitoring
topics.description: You should now implement monitoring and autorestarts for your application using monit. Regarding autorestarts, you can also use [pm2](https://pm2.keymetrics.io/).

topics.name: Checkpoint / Simple Crud Apps
topics.description: **CRUD** stands for **Create, Read, Update, and Delete**. These are the four basic operations you can perform on any data when working with web applications, databases, and APIs.

topics.name: Css
topics.description: CSS or Cascading Style Sheets is the language used to style the frontend of any website. CSS is a cornerstone technology of the World Wide Web, alongside HTML and JavaScript.

topics.name: Ec2
topics.description: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) is a web service that provides resizable compute capacity in the form of virtual servers, known as instances. With EC2, you can quickly scale your infrastructure up or down as your computing requirements change. Thi

links.title: Up and Running with AWS EC2
Deploy Node App on AWS EC2

topics.name: Git
topics.description: Git is a free and open source distributed version control system designed to handle everything from small to very large projects with speed and efficiency.

links.title: Learn Git with Tutorials, News and Tips - Atlassian

topics.name: Github Actions
topics.description: GitHub Actions is a workflow automation tool provided by GitHub that can be used to automate various tasks in the app development process.

topics.name: Github
topics.description: GitHub is a provider of Internet hosting for software development and version control using Git. It offers the distributed version control and source code management functionality of Git, plus its own features.

links.title: How to Use Git in a Professional Dev Team
Git vs. GitHub: Whats the difference?

topics.name: Html
topics.description: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It is used on the frontend and gives the structure to the webpage which you can style using CSS and make interactive using JavaScript.

links.title: HTML Full Course - Build a Website Tutorial

topics.name: Javascript
topics.description: JavaScript allows you to add interactivity to your pages. Common examples that you may have seen on the websites are sliders, click interactions, popups and so on.

topics.name: Jwt Auth
topics.description: JWT stands for JSON Web Token is a token-based encryption open standard/methodology that is used to transfer information securely as a JSON object. Clients and Servers use JWT to securely share information, with the JWT containing encoded JSON object

links.title: What Is JWT and Why Should You Use JWT

topics.name: Linux Basics
topics.description: Knowledge of UNIX is a must for almost all kind of development as most of the code that you write is most likely going to be finally deployed on a UNIX/Linux machine. Linux has been the backbone of the free and open source software movement, providin

topics.name: Monit

topics.name: Nodejs
topics.description: Node.js is an open-source and cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment. It is a popular tool for almost any kind of project! Node.js runs the V8 JavaScript engine, Google Chrome's core, outside the browser. This allows Node.js to be very perform

topics.name: Npm
topics.description: npm is a package manager for the JavaScript programming language maintained by npm, Inc. npm is the default package manager for the JavaScript runtime environment Node.js.

links.title: An Absolute Beginners Guide to Using npm

topics.name: Postgresql
topics.description: PostgreSQL, also known as Postgres, is a free and open-source relational database management system emphasizing extensibility and SQL compliance.

links.title: Learn PostgreSQL - Full Tutorial for Beginners
Learn PostgreSQL Tutorial - Full Course for Beginners

topics.name: React
topics.description: React is the most popular front-end JavaScript library for building user interfaces. React can also render on the server using Node and power mobile apps using React Native.

topics.name: Redis
topics.description: Redis is an open source (BSD licensed), in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. Redis provides data structures such as strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets with range queries, bitmaps, hype

topics.name: Restful Apis
topics.description: REST, or REpresentational State Transfer, is an architectural style for providing standards between computer systems on the web, making it easier for systems to communicate with each other.

links.title: Roy Fieldings dissertation chapter, Representational State Transfer (REST)
What Is A RESTful API? Explanation of REST & HTTP

topics.name: Route53
topics.description: Route53 is AWS's Domain Name System (DNS) service that plays a critical role in connecting user requests to your web application or other resources within your infrastructure. With Route53, you can easily manage domains, redirect traffic, and configu

topics.name: S3
topics.description: S3 is a service that allows you to store files in the cloud. It's a simple service that you can use to store files and serve them to your users.

links.title: Upload Images to S3 from Node Back End
S3 Bucket Hosting a Static Website

topics.name: Ses
topics.description: Amazon SES (Simple Email Service) is a scalable, flexible, and cost-effective cloud-based email service that is specifically designed for developers, marketers, and businesses to send and receive marketing, transactional, and notification emails. SES

links.title: Contact Form Submission With AWS SES

topics.name: Tailwind Css
topics.description: CSS Framework that provides atomic CSS classes to help you style components e.g. `flex`, `pt-4`, `text-center` and `rotate-90` that can be composed to build any design, directly in your markup.

links.title: Tailwind CSS Full Course for Beginners

topics.name: Terraform

topics.name: Vpc
